question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/controller/AccountController.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a 'long-zero' EVM address (first 12 bytes all zero, last 8 bytes encoding a target account num) via the evmAddress input to cause getByEvmAddressAndType() to resolve to a completely different account than intended, returning that account's freeze/receiverSigRequired state and enabling the attacker to identify and exploit accounts whose funds are effectively frozen?
